Prompt

Oracle Events
OE1: Look up Anh Nguyen's own contact details using `contacts_search_contacts` (query: "Anh" or "Anh Nguyen") to confirm sender email (anh.nguyen@moveops.com) for outgoing emails.
OE2: Search emails for dashboard-related messages using `search_emails` (query: "dashboard" or "expense dashboard" or "walkthrough" or "bulk export" or "readiness" or similar) to discover emails from David Chen, Marcus Thorne, Chloe Vance, Samira Tariq, and Dmitri Volkov.
OE3: Search emails for Dmitri's reply about duplicate detection using `search_emails` (query: "duplicate" or "DuplicateDetector" or "ENG-220" or "schema" or similar) to find Dmitri's warning about the duplicate_status field.
OE4: Search Slack for engineering channel discussions about launch readiness using `conversations_search_messages` (search_query: "readiness" or "launch checklist" or "ENG-219" or "ENG-218" or similar) to find Samira's readiness thread and Dmitri's dashboard gap warning.
OE5: Pull #engineering channel history using `conversations_history` (channel_id: "C003") for the full readiness discussion thread including Anh's own progress update and Dmitri's reply.
OE6: Read the #engineering thread replies using `conversations_replies` (channel_id: "C003", thread_ts: "1774135800.000000"). The agent needs to discover two critical replies: (1) Anh's own reply (ts: 1774136700.000000) stating ENG-218 is about 80% done with numeric validation and array serialization working, remaining work is the Safari edge case for single excluded_category serialization, expected done by Monday; ENG-213 bulk export still backlog and not started; ENG-208 rule config UI stable in staging. Samira reacted with :eyes:. (2) Dmitri's reply (ts: 1774368900.000000) stating the duplicate_status field from ENG-220 causes "undefined" in the dashboard review queue, it is a 2-3 hour fix for Anh but IS blocking launch readiness, and that he filed a detailed comment on ENG-213 with full integration requirements.
OE7: List Anh's assigned Linear issues using `linear_list_issues` (assignee: "moveops_anh_nguyen" or project: "Expense Auto-Categorization" or similar) to see all tickets.
OE8: Retrieve individual issue details for Anh's key tickets using `linear_get_issue` (id: "issue_dashboard_type_validation" for ENG-218, "issue_bulk_export" for ENG-213, "issue_rule_config_ui" for ENG-208, and optionally "issue_launch_readiness_checklist" for ENG-219 or "issue_stipend_duplicate_detection" for ENG-220).
OE9: List comments on ENG-213 (bulk export) using `linear_list_comments` (issueId: "issue_bulk_export") to find Dmitri's detailed comment about the duplicate_status integration requirements and CSV schema fields.
OE10: Search Airtable stipend records for flagged employees using `airtable_search_records` (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Stipend Transactions", field_name: "Employee Name", value: "Kevin Tran") and/or (value: "Andre Washington") and/or (value: "Jordan Ekwueme") to check the data issues referenced in Dmitri's duplicate detection work.
OE11: Search QuickBooks for stipend payment holds using `quickbooks_search_bills` (criteria matching "STIP" or "stipend" or "TechComfort" or similar) to find the pending payment holds (STIP-2026-0094, STIP-2026-0097) Marcus referenced in his export request.
OE12: Check Anh's calendar availability using `calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to` (start_datetime and end_datetime covering the week of March 25-28 and/or the week of March 31) to identify open slots for David's CE walkthrough.
OE13: Look up Samira Tariq's email using `contacts_search_contacts` (query: "Samira" or "Samira Tariq") for the readiness report recipient.
OE14: Look up David Chen's email using `contacts_search_contacts` (query: "David Chen" or "David") for the walkthrough scheduling reply.
OE15: Look up Marcus Thorne's email using `contacts_search_contacts` (query: "Marcus" or "Marcus Thorne") for the export feature reply.
OE16: Look up Chloe Vance's email using `contacts_search_contacts` (query: "Chloe" or "Chloe Vance") for the feature request reply.
OE17: Update stale Linear tickets with current status using `linear_update_issue` (id: "issue_dashboard_type_validation" and/or "issue_bulk_export" and/or others, with description or state updates reflecting investigation findings — e.g., ENG-218 approximately 80% done, ENG-213 has new integration dependencies from ENG-220).
OE18: Create new Linear issue(s) for Chloe's feature requests using `linear_create_issue` (team: "team_engineering", title and description covering relocation context column and/or cancelled move indicator, project: "proj_expense_autocategorizer" or similar).
OE19: Send Samira Tariq a status update using `send_email` (sender: anh.nguyen@moveops.com, recipients: [samira.tariq@moveops.com], content covering ENG-218 progress, duplicate_status gap as launch blocker, bulk export status, and new incoming requests from ops/finance/CE).
OE20: Reply to David Chen about the CE walkthrough using `send_email` or `reply_to_email` (sender: anh.nguyen@moveops.com, recipients or email_id for David's email, content including available times from the calendar for the week of March 31).
OE21: Reply to Marcus Thorne about the bulk export using `send_email` or `reply_to_email` (sender: anh.nguyen@moveops.com, recipients or email_id for Marcus's email, content acknowledging the request and that Anh is looking into it).
OE22: Reply to Chloe Vance about her feature requests using `send_email` or `reply_to_email` (sender: anh.nguyen@moveops.com, recipients or email_id for Chloe's email, content acknowledging both requests and confirming tickets have been filed).
OE23: Search or pull #operations Slack channel history using `conversations_search_messages` (search_query: "dashboard" or "expense" or "relocation" or similar) and/or `conversations_history` (channel_id: "C006") to investigate whether there are dashboard-related discussions in a channel Anh does not normally check. The prompt says "conversations happening in channels I do not normally check where people are talking about issues with my dashboard work." This channel primarily contains relocation logistics (apartment sourcing, vendor disputes, volume updates) — Chloe's specific dashboard feature requests came via email rather than Slack, but the investigative step is important for due diligence.
OE24: Search SENT emails using `search_emails` (query: "export" or "CSV" or "Marcus" or "reconciliation" or similar, folder_name: "SENT") to check for prior outbound commitments. The prompt says "I have a vague memory of promising someone outside engineering I would deliver something and I am worried that deadline already passed." The SENT folder contains email_anh_dmitri_schema_0321 (Anh's email to Dmitri about ENG-218 type validation alignment from March 19). No separate promise email to someone outside engineering exists in the data, but the search is necessary due diligence. Marcus's inbox email and the prompt context suggest a prior verbal or informal commitment about the export.
OE25: Search or pull #finance Slack channel history using `conversations_search_messages` (search_query: "export" or "reconciliation" or "audit" or "stipend" or similar) and/or `conversations_history` (channel_id: "C005") to investigate finance-related discussions about the dashboard or export. The #finance channel contains invoice posts from Hana Kim and a Marcus post about STIP payment holds (STIP-2026-0094 and STIP-2026-0097 totaling $134), providing additional context for the export urgency. There is no specific Marcus-Hana dialogue about the export feature itself, but the channel provides financial context relevant to Marcus's request.
OE26: List comments on ENG-220 using `linear_list_comments` (issueId: "issue_stipend_duplicate_detection") to discover Dmitri's comment about the hard dependency on ENG-217 pipeline validation gate and the 3-layer interdependency chain.
OE27: Search Airtable relocation records using `airtable_search_records` (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Kevin Tran" or "Andre Washington" or similar) to cross-reference flagged stipend employees with their relocation status, relevant to Chloe's cancelled move indicator request.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 An email was sent (via send_email or reply_to_email) to samira.tariq@moveops.com containing a status update on Anh's dashboard components, and the tool returned success.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called reply_to_email with email_id 'email_samira_eng_readiness_0321' and sender 'anh.nguyen@moveops.com'. The tool returned a success response with an email_id. The email_id indicates this was Samira's readiness email, making her the recipient.
✓Run #2The agent called reply_to_email with email_id 'email_samira_eng_readiness_0321' from anh.nguyen@moveops.com. The tool returned a success response with a new email_id. The email is directed to Samira and contains a full component readiness status update.
✓Run #3The agent called reply_to_email with email_id 'email_samira_eng_readiness_0321' and sender 'anh.nguyen@moveops.com'. The tool returned a success response with an email_id. The email_id clearly references Samira's engineering readiness email.
✓Run #4The trajectory shows a send_email call with recipients=['samira.tariq@moveops.com'] that returned a successful email_id ('10ff2e2525684329a5d835ad6967f402').
✓Run #5A reply_to_email was sent to email_id 'email_samira_eng_readiness_0321' from anh.nguyen@moveops.com with a detailed readiness report. The tool returned success with a new email_id. The email was addressed to Samira Tariq.
✓Run #6The agent called reply_to_email with email_id 'email_samira_eng_readiness_0321' (Samira's readiness email), and the tool returned success with a new email_id.
2 The status update email body (in the send_email or reply_to_email content parameter) states that ENG-218 (dashboard type validation) is approximately 80% complete, with the remaining work being a browser edge case for excluded category array serialization.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✗✗✓ 67%
✓Run #1The email body to Samira explicitly states 'Status: ~80% complete' and details 'The remaining work is a Safari-specific edge case where single-item multi-select serializes as a string instead of an array.'
✓Run #2The Samira email body explicitly states '~80% complete as of my last update' and describes 'the Safari single-item multi-select bug where a single excluded category serializes as a string instead of an array' as the remaining browser edge case.
✓Run #3The email body to Samira states '~80% complete, deployed to staging' and explicitly notes: 'Remaining item: edge case where a single excluded category gets serialized as a string instead of an array in Safari.' This matches the criterion exactly.
✗Run #4The email to Samira states ENG-218 is '~90% complete' (not ~80% as specified in the criterion). The Safari/browser edge case for array serialization is correctly described, but the completion percentage does not match the ~80% figure from the Slack thread.
✗Run #5The email to Samira states ENG-218 is '~95% complete' and mentions the Safari edge case as remaining. The criterion requires 'approximately 80% complete' with the browser edge case. The agent used 95% instead of the 80% figure from the Slack thread.
✓Run #6The email body to Samira explicitly states ENG-218 is '~80% complete' and identifies the remaining work as 'Safari-specific edge case where a single excluded category serializes as a string instead of an array.'
3 The status update email body (in the send_email or reply_to_email content parameter) notes that Dmitri's DuplicateDetector (ENG-220) adds a duplicate_status field that the dashboard review queue currently displays as 'undefined'.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email to Samira explicitly states: 'Dmitri flagged that the review queue renders "undefined" for the new duplicate_status field. This is a launch readiness blocker.' The DuplicateDetector / ENG-220 connection is also stated.
✓Run #2The Samira email body states: 'Dmitri's DuplicateDetector adds a duplicate_status field that the review queue does not render yet — it currently shows "undefined".' This directly matches the criterion.
✓Run #3The email to Samira explicitly states under 'NEW BLOCKER': 'Dmitri's DuplicateDetector work adds a duplicate_status field to the expense response schema. The dashboard review queue currently renders "undefined" for this field.'
✓Run #4The email to Samira explicitly states: 'Dmitri's DuplicateDetector adds a duplicate_status field to the expense response schema. The dashboard review queue currently renders "undefined" for this field.' This directly meets the criterion.
✓Run #5The email to Samira explicitly states: 'the dashboard review queue currently renders "undefined" for it' in reference to the duplicate_status field from Dmitri's DuplicateDetector (ENG-220). This matches the criterion exactly.
✓Run #6The email body to Samira explicitly states 'dashboard review queue renders "undefined" for the new `duplicate_status` field from his DuplicateDetector work.'
4 The status update email body (in the send_email or reply_to_email content parameter) addresses the bulk export feature (ENG-213), stating it has not been started or is still in backlog.
outcome

✓Run #2The agent called linear_update_issue with id 'issue_dashboard_type_validation'. The description update explicitly states '~80% complete' and describes 'Safari single-item multi-select edge case where a single excluded category serializes as a string instead of an array' as the remaining work.
✗Run #3The agent called linear_update_issue for issue_bulk_export (ENG-213) and issue_rule_config_ui (ENG-208), but never called linear_update_issue for issue_dashboard_type_validation (ENG-218). No ticket update was made to ENG-218 with progress status or the browser edge case.
✗Run #4The linear_update_issue call for ENG-218 states '~90% complete' and mentions the Safari serialization edge case. While the browser edge case detail is correct, the criterion specifies approximately 80% (the figure from the Slack thread). The agent reported 90%, which doesn't match.
✗Run #5The linear_update_issue call for 'issue_dashboard_type_validation' (ENG-218) updated the description to say '~95% complete' with the Safari edge case remaining. ✗Run #6The agent updated issue_bulk_export and issue_rule_config_ui via linear_update_issue, but never called linear_update_issue for issue_dashboard_type_validation (ENG-218) to add progress information.
